extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r2 (asthma AND diabetes); rec_drug_b is set true only by r3 (asthma). Both target distinct outputs with no competing rules, so priorities/ties are irrelevant for these two outputs. With asthma=true and diabetes=true: r2 fires setting rec_drug_a:=true, and r3 fires setting rec_drug_b:=true. bradycardia and seizure_history only affect rec_drug_c and are set false. This yields rec_drug_a=true AND rec_drug_b=true, violating the mutual-exclusion property (no input may make rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b jointly true).
